name: Public Sector / GovTech (US)
description: Curated framework bundle for vendors selling to US federal, state and local government — including SaaS that processes Federal Information, cloud services seeking FedRAMP authorisation, and AI tools subject to OMB M-24-10 / M-25-21.

crosswalk_pairs.rationale: CSF 2.0 GV / ID / PR / DE / RS / RC functions cross-walk to ISO 27001 Clause 4.1 + Annex A.5 / A.8 — many federal-customer security questionnaires accept either as the underlying control catalogue.
ISO 27001 Annex A controls evidence the SOC 2 Common Criteria — SOC 2 Type II is the de-facto minimum for state-and-local procurement and a useful pre-FedRAMP exercise.
If the AI capability is also offered to EU public-sector customers, the AI RMF MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE evidence reads onto EU AI Act Art. 9 risk-management for high-risk public-sector AI.

sequencing_notes: 1. NIST CSF 2.0 first as the organising taxonomy — every federal counterparty speaks CSF, and CSF 2.0 GV (Govern) function is the bridge to programme-level governance.

2. NIST AI RMF 1.0 for any AI capability — federal AI inventories under OMB M-24-10 + M-25-21 expect AI-RMF-aligned documentation; the OSCAL-emit companion in @regunav/ontology can produce machine-readable bundles.
3. SOC 2 Type II for commercial-flavour state / local procurement and as evidence pack for the FedRAMP 3PAO assessment.

4. FedRAMP authorisation track — Moderate or High baseline depending on data sensitivity; OSCAL SSP + SAP + SAR + POA&M are the deliverable shape. Use the OSCAL emitter to bootstrap the SSP from your ISO 27001 Statement of Applicability.
5. ISO 27001 is not required by the US government, but it considerably accelerates the FedRAMP control-implementation work because Annex A mostly subsets NIST SP 800-53 control families.
6. HIPAA only if VA / IHS / DoD-MHS or state Medicaid / public-health data is in scope. PCI DSS only if government-payment-portal cardholder data is processed.
